title: phase 01: freeze compatibility contract
description: Capture the current Python behavior as the rewrite contract: command inventory, golden CLI fixtures, trace fixtures, performance budgets, and acceptance tests for GitHub delegation plus Forgejo compatibility.
notes:
Implementation order 01. Blocks every Rust code phase. No parallel implementation starts until this contract exists.
Subagents: use explorer subagents for command-surface inventory, config/auth/setup inventory, and trace/smoke/git-recorder inventory. One integrator owns final fixtures.
close_reason: phase 01 contract PR #17 merged after review: compatibility-contract.v1 fixtures, contract README, fixture tests, baseline timings, and reviewer repair commit 937bc4b are on main

title: epic: full Rust rewrite of gh-forgejo-shim runtime
description: Full Rust rewrite of gh-forgejo-shim so the installed gh, gfj, and gh-forgejo-shim runtime paths no longer depend on Python. The target is zero Python in the final product. Any proposed retained Python after phase 10 requires a separate decision bead and cannot be installed runtime code. This epic coordinates the child implementation phases and their dependency graph.
notes: Zero-Python target: Rust is the final installed runtime. Any retained Python after phase 10 requires a separate decision bead and cannot be installed runtime code.

title: planning: document Rust rewrite implementation plan
description: Create docs/implementation/rust-rewrite with phase documents, IMPLEMENT.md, PLAN.html, and beads phase tracking for a full Rust rewrite that removes Python from the gh runtime path.
notes: Planning docs live under docs/implementation/rust-rewrite. This planning bead is not an implementation phase.
close_reason: Created docs/implementation/rust-rewrite with IMPLEMENT.md, PLAN.html, phase documents, zero-Python target language, epic/phase bead links, dependencies, priorities, and subagent guidance.

title: Make generated gh shim independent of PATH
description: Generate the gh wrapper so GUI apps can run it even when gh-forgejo-shim is not discoverable through PATH, fixing Codex.app GitHub CLI unavailable detection.
close_reason: Generated gh wrapper now invokes gh_forgejo_shim via the install-time Python executable, so it works in GUI app environments with stripped PATH.

title: repair CI discovery tests for hosted gh
description: Reviewer repair for PR 25: Rust and legacy Python discovery tests fail on GitHub Actions runners because /usr/bin/gh is present. Isolate the tests from host-installed gh while preserving the Phase 09 packaging/release scope.
close_reason: Repaired PR 25 CI by isolating Rust and Python discovery tests from host-installed /usr/bin/gh, and removed stale Python-release guidance from contributing docs. Verified Rust, Python, packaging, workflow, shell, Ruby, and diff checks locally.

title: phase 06 reviewer: enforce missing auth for read-only rust routes
description: The Rust read-only managed-gh executor only returns the shim login/import guidance for auth token and api user. The compatibility contract requires routed repo/pr/issue API calls without Forgejo auth to fail with the same guidance, while out-of-scope mutating commands must still report unsupported command errors.
close_reason: enforced missing-auth guidance for Rust read-only repo/pr/issue routes while preserving unsupported mutating command errors; verified with focused and full gates

title: review phase 04 config writes should ignore env overrides
description: Reviewer repair for Phase 04: keep FJ_SHIM_HOSTS and FJ_SHIM_REAL_* as current-process overrides and prevent Rust config/auth management writes from persisting those temporary values into config.toml.
close_reason: review repairs implemented and verified with fmt, cargo test, clippy, and Python unittest suite

title: harden phase 03 rust trace redaction
description: Reviewer repair for Phase 03: the Rust dispatcher trace redaction must not leak token-like strings or sensitive key/value arguments, and trace output should be written for local trace paths as well as nested paths.
close_reason: Hardened Rust dispatcher trace redaction for sensitive key/value text, token prefixes, and URL credentials; fixed local FJ_SHIM_TRACE paths; verified with cargo fmt, clippy, cargo test, pytest, release build, and diff check.

title: review phase 04 auth file temp permissions
description: Reviewer repair for Phase 04: ensure Rust auth fallback file writes never create token-bearing temp files with permissive default permissions before chmod.
close_reason: review repairs implemented and verified with fmt, cargo test, clippy, and Python unittest suite

title: repair phase 02 rust scaffold review findings
description: Review PR #18 for phase 02 of the Rust rewrite and make narrow scaffold repairs needed before merge.
notes: Reviewer repair for PR #18 on branch lavender/rust-phase-02-workspace. Keep scope to scaffold/test harness; do not wire Rust into installed Python paths.
close_reason: review repair complete: removed hard-coded Rust version assertions, strengthened the CLI fixture harness for fake executables and system git access, documented the harness behavior, and reran Rust/Python quality gates

title: review phase 01 compatibility contract PR
description: Review PR #17 for phase 01 Rust rewrite contract correctness, repair any contract/test gaps, run quality gates, and report status back to the orchestrator thread.
close_reason: review repaired PR contract fixture coverage and verified tests

title: phase 03: native dispatcher and GitHub delegation
description: Implement the fast Rust gh entry path: argv parsing, real gh discovery, repo/host detection, allowlist routing, process exec/delegation, and minimal trace records for delegated GitHub commands.
notes: Implemented native Rust managed-gh dispatcher for Phase 03: supported command family classification, repo/host detection from -R/--repo, URLs, GH_REPO, GH_HOST+git remote, and git remotes; real gh discovery with managed-shim avoidance; GitHub/unsupported delegation; native Forgejo route selection placeholder for later command phases; minimal redacted trace JSONL for delegated gh commands. Verification: cargo fmt --all; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; /tmp/gh-forgejo-shim-pytest-8d10/bin/python -m pytest (132 passed); cargo build --release --bin gh-forgejo-shim; timing with FJ_SHIM_REAL_GH=/opt/homebrew/bin/gh over 15 runs: real gh median 51.9ms, Rust dispatcher median 27.8ms, Python shim median 486.9ms.
close_reason: Phase 03 native dispatcher and GitHub delegation implemented, verified, and ready for review.

title: phase 02: establish Rust workspace
description: Create the Cargo workspace, module layout, error/result conventions, test harness, fixture runner, and binary skeletons without changing the installed user workflow yet.
notes:
Implementation order 02. Depends on phase 01. Creates the workspace and skeleton needed by all porting work.
Subagents: avoid parallel implementers. One owner creates Cargo layout; optionally use one verifier after scaffold exists.
close_reason: phase 02 scaffold PR #18 merged after review: Cargo workspace, Rust package/binaries, CLI fixture harness, contract loader, and scaffold docs are on main at d7eacd0

title: Add delegated GitHub PR create head fallback
description: Codex can show pull request creation unavailable on a GitHub checkout because real gh pr create without --head exits with 'you must first push the current branch to a remote' even when the current branch tracks an existing origin branch. Add a narrow compatibility fallback for delegated GitHub pr create commands so a pushed current branch gets an explicit --head argument.

title: Fix pipx-installed CLI startup failure
description: After reinstalling this package with pipx install --force ., both gfj --help and gh-forgejo-shim doctor fail. Diagnose the installed console scripts and fix the package entry points or imports so pipx-installed commands start correctly.
close_reason: Could not reproduce after inspecting installed pipx scripts; refreshed the pipx install and reinstalled the managed gh shim with --force. gfj --help and gh-forgejo-shim doctor now pass.

title: Fix Codex GitHub CLI unavailable regression
description: Codex.app is reporting GitHub CLI Unavailable when using both Forgejo and GitHub repositories through gfj/gh-forgejo-shim. Diagnose whether the installed gfj version is current, reproduce the availability-detection failure, and update the shim or docs/tests so Codex can detect the CLI again.
close_reason: fixed Codex availability regression by routing Forgejo auth/API identity probes through the shim, installed 0.1.1 locally, and validated GitHub delegation still works

title: Handle missing Forgejo token on PR create
description: Codex.app gh pr create against git.dirtydishes.dev can reach the Forgejo API without an Authorization header and gets HTTP 401 token is required. Investigate token discovery for PR creation, make the failure actionable or restore token lookup, and add regression coverage for the behavior.
close_reason: Added host-specific auth guarding for Forgejo write commands, made doctor report missing tokens per configured host, documented the behavior, and validated with focused plus full unittest runs.

title: Make Codex app gh discovery durable
description: Codex.app is showing GitHub CLI unavailable across Forgejo and GitHub repositories. Investigate the gh wrapper and GUI discovery paths, then make the shim resilient so Codex can reliably find and run gh.
close_reason: Implemented durable Codex gh discovery repair, installed the updated shim locally, and validated GitHub plus Forgejo workflows.

title: Restore GitHub PR info passthrough
description: Codex.app currently shows PR info unavailable for GitHub repositories while Forgejo repositories still resolve PR info. Forgejo repositories such as IslandFlow also no longer expose clickable PR links in Codex.app. Investigate shim routing, delegation, and GitHub-shaped PR/repo JSON fields so native GitHub calls pass through and Forgejo PR responses keep usable URL metadata.
close_reason: Fixed GitHub host delegation and Forgejo PR URL template output; added regression tests and documentation.

title: Teach bootstrap to catch missing macOS GUI shim PATH
description: After reinstalling the shim with gfj bootstrap, Codex can still miss Forgejo PR status if the macOS launchd GUI PATH excludes ~/.local/bin. Bootstrap should report this gap and print the install-gui-path repair command instead of appearing fully successful.
close_reason: Bootstrap now checks macOS launchd GUI PATH, prints install-gui-path when Codex cannot see the shim, and has regression coverage.

title: Keep GitHub CLI discoverable after uninstalling shim
description: Uninstalling the Forgejo gh shim should not make Codex or other macOS GUI apps lose access to the real GitHub CLI. The uninstall flow needs to leave launchd PATH in a usable state and tests should cover the fallback.
close_reason: macOS uninstall now rewrites shim-owned GUI PATH helpers to a real GitHub CLI fallback, applies launchd PATH, and has regression coverage.

title: Catch stale Forgejo host allowlists in doctor
description: Codex.app PR creation in islandflow delegated to the real GitHub CLI because the checkout now detects as git.dirtydishes.dev while the shim config only allowlisted the old git.deltaisland.io host. Doctor reported ok because it only checked that some Forgejo host existed, not that the current repository host was allowlisted. Update diagnostics and coverage so stale per-repo allowlists are visible before PR creation fails.
close_reason: Repaired islandflow routing by allowlisting git.dirtydishes.dev locally, then added a repo-aware doctor check and tests so stale Forgejo host allowlists are reported with an exact repair command.

title: Reconcile divergent Beads Dolt remote history
description: bd dolt pull failed because local and remote Dolt histories had no common ancestor. Compare local and remote Beads data, choose the authoritative history, repair the remote, and verify pull/push work again.
close_reason: Repaired the divergent Dolt remote by confirming the remote was an empty initialized history, force-pushing the local authoritative Beads history, and verifying bd dolt pull plus a clean remote clone.

title: Fix desktop sidebar terminal store reference
description: Desktop launch reaches the UI shell but crashes in Sidebar because useTerminalStateStore is referenced without being defined. Investigate the missing store reference, fix the runtime crash, and validate the desktop/web build path enough to confirm the Sidebar renders again.
close_reason: Closed as misfiled: the reported Sidebar crash was in /Users/kell/dev/oss-playground/dirtycode, not this repository.

title: Fix Codex PR board compatibility for Forgejo shim
description: Codex.app probes gh pr list with GitHub-shaped PR board fields, including additions, deletions, mergeable, statusCheckRollup, and --state merged. The shim currently emits null/missing or non-GitHub-shaped values for some of these fields and rejects merged state, causing Codex to show pull request status unavailable after checking PR status. Normalize these fields and add minimal checks support so Codex can parse Forgejo PR status.
close_reason: fixed Codex.app PR board compatibility by normalizing PR board fields, accepting merged PR lists, and adding parse-safe pr checks output

title: Shim gh pr list and repo view for Forgejo repos
description: Codex.app reports GitHub CLI unavailable because gh pr list and gh repo view still fall through to stock gh in Forgejo-only repositories. Add supported shim behavior for those commands so local Forgejo PR metadata can be discovered through the gh-compatible wrapper.
close_reason: Added Forgejo-backed gh pr list and gh repo view routing, tests, docs, and refreshed the active pipx shim.

title: Fix Codex PR status detection for Forgejo shim
description: Codex.app still reports pull request status unavailable after detecting the shim. Investigate the gh pr status/view compatibility surface and make the Forgejo shim return the GitHub CLI-compatible output Codex expects.
close_reason: Fixed gh pr status JSON envelope compatibility for Codex.app PR detection.

title: Discover fj macOS auth config
description: Teach auth discovery to read forgejo-cli's macOS Application Support keys.json so GUI apps like Codex can find Forgejo tokens without inherited shell environment variables.
close_reason: Auth discovery now reads fj's macOS Application Support keys.json, allowing Codex.app to find Forgejo auth without inherited token env vars.

title: Build gh-forgejo-shim v1 package
description: Implement the initial stdlib-only Python CLI package, gh wrapper lifecycle, Forgejo PR routing, tests, docs, and turn documentation from the OSS project plan.
close_reason: Implemented the V1 package, CLI wrapper lifecycle, Forgejo PR routing, docs, tests, and turn documentation.

title: Document pipx-to-Rust migration
description: Add migration documentation for existing pipx users moving to the Rust-first installation path, including doctor verification, pipx removal, rollback behavior, and PyPI distribution expectations.
close_reason: Documented pipx-to-Rust migration path, verification, uninstall, rollback, and PyPI channel expectations.

title: repair rust git recorder streaming behavior
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: Phase 08 Rust git recorder currently buffers real git stdout/stderr until process exit and passes null stdin, which diverges from the Python recorder contract that streams real git output and inherits stdin. Repair the hidden recorder runner to stream stdout/stderr while collecting byte counts/excerpts, keep stdin inherited, and verify with focused tests.
close_reason: rust git recorder now inherits stdin and streams stdout/stderr while preserving trace byte and excerpt recording

title: Investigate trace git-recorder integration hang
description: The full Rust cli_scaffold integration run hangs in trace_git_recorder_captures_fake_git_and_removes_wrapper after the doctor/bootstrap changes are passing. Reproduce with cargo test -p gh-forgejo-shim --test cli_scaffold; targeted doctor/bootstrap tests pass. This appears owned by Phase 08 trace git-recorder work rather than doctor/bootstrap.
close_reason: fixed git-recorder run deadlock by bypassing CLI stdout locks for the hidden wrapper runner; cli_scaffold recorder test now passes

title: phase 08 worker c trace rust parity
description: Implement Worker C Phase 08 diagnostics scope: trace summarize, trace smoke, and trace git-recorder create/remove behavior in the Rust CLI while preserving recorder safety and raw git probe capture/removal behavior. Use existing Python behavior, Rust trace helpers, and contract fixtures; avoid real user environment mutation by testing with temp homes/bins and fake git/gh.
close_reason: Implemented Rust trace summarize, trace smoke, and git recorder create/run/remove with temp-only tests and recorder safety checks.

title: phase 08 worker d rust gui path launchagent commands
description: Implement the Worker D portion of Phase 08: port macOS GUI PATH LaunchAgent install-gui-path and uninstall-gui-path behavior to Rust with isolated tests that use temp HOME/Library/LaunchAgents and fake launchctl boundaries.
close_reason: implemented Rust macOS GUI PATH LaunchAgent helpers and CLI wiring with isolated temp-home/fake-launchctl tests

title: preserve rust web launch behavior for mutating workflows
description: Rust Phase 07 web-mode handlers currently print URLs but do not launch the browser like the Python contract. Preserve --web behavior for PR create/comment and issue create while keeping the printed URL output.
close_reason: Rust mutating --web workflows now launch browser URLs before printing them, matching the Python contract, with focused tests.

title: Port auth login import logout writes to Rust
description: Phase 07 worker D owns the Rust write behavior for auth login, auth import, and auth logout. Preserve token validation, prompt/stdin safety, config write and removal semantics, and clear failures using fake Forgejo servers and temp config homes only.
close_reason: implemented Rust auth login/import/logout write hardening with focused tests; full Rust crate and Python auth parity tests pass on clean auth branch

title: Phase 07 Rust PR checkout and git subprocess behavior
description: Worker C owns the Phase 07 Rust rewrite surface for PR checkout/co and git subprocess behavior. Preserve Python-compatible error behavior around dirty local changes, branch creation, remotes/fetch, and repository detection while using existing Rust auth/config, HTTP normalization, routing, and execution interfaces. Tests must use fake Forgejo servers and temporary git repos only.
notes: Implemented Rust PR checkout/co against existing Phase 07 handler architecture. Added shared external::git_run with Python-compatible captured stderr/stdout and OS-error exit 127 shape. Added checkout tests with fake Forgejo API data and temporary local git repos covering head ref fetch, refs/pull fallback, and dirty worktree checkout failure preservation. Verified cargo check -p gh-forgejo-shim, cargo test -p gh-forgejo-shim, focused checkout tests, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented Rust PR checkout/co and git subprocess behavior with temp-repo tests; full gh-forgejo-shim Rust tests pass.

title: Phase 07 Rust PR comment and issue create/new
description: Implement the Phase 07 Rust rewrite surface for PR comment plus issue create/new while preserving existing Python behavior and using fake Forgejo servers/temp repos for tests.
close_reason: Implemented Rust PR comment and issue create/new handlers with stdin body-file support, auth/title/body failures, fake API unit coverage, and cargo test -p gh-forgejo-shim passing.

title: phase 06: port read-only gh compatibility
description: Port Codex-critical read-only commands to Rust: auth status/token, api user, repo view, pr list/status/view/checks/diff, issue list/view, and JSON/text output parity.
notes:
Implementation order 06. Depends on phases 03, 04, and 05. This is the first broad Forgejo runtime parity milestone.
Subagents: split by read-only command family after shared helpers are stable; use a separate verifier for Codex smoke and parity.
close_reason: implemented Rust read-only gh compatibility handlers for auth/api/repo/pr/issue families; verified with cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace, python3 -m unittest, python3 -m compileall -q src tests, and Rust-path auth/delegation smoke probes

title: phase 05: port Forgejo HTTP and normalization core
description: Port the Forgejo HTTP client, response models, GitHub-shaped normalization, field filtering, jq/template-compatible output helpers, and error mapping to Rust.
notes:
Implementation order 05. Depends on phase 02. Can run in parallel with phases 03 and 04.
Subagents: split Forgejo HTTP client, normalization, and field/jq/template helpers after shared value types are stable.
close_reason: implemented Rust Forgejo HTTP client, normalization, field filtering, jq/template helpers, and contract fixture coverage; cargo test, clippy, and python unittest pass

title: phase 04: port config and auth
description: Port config loading/writing, host normalization, Forgejo token discovery, auth storage, env/fj/tea/gitea import, macOS Keychain integration, and auth command behavior to Rust.
notes:
Implementation order 04. Depends on phase 02. Can run in parallel with phases 03 and 05.

Completed Rust config/auth port: config TOML load/write, host normalization, env overrides, token discovery from env/shim storage/external fj/tea/gitea files, macOS Keychain integration with auth.json fallback mode 0600, and config/auth management CLI commands. Verification: cargo fmt -- --check; cargo test; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings; PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover tests.
close_reason: phase 04 config/auth Rust port implemented and verified

title: document codex trace adaptation workflow
description: Add a polished HTML testing guide that explains how to capture Codex gh/git traces, summarize them, and convert observed behavior into shim compatibility tests and fixes.
close_reason: Added docs/testing/codex-trace.html with the Codex trace capture, summary, git recorder, test conversion, and shim adaptation workflow.

title: Add opt-in trace record module
description: Implement the core stdlib-only trace record module and focused unit tests without wiring it into routing yet.
close_reason: Implemented trace module and focused unit tests.

title: Implement temporary git recorder core
description: Add an isolated stdlib-only git recorder wrapper helper that can create and remove a temporary wrapper for one Codex launch session without wiring it into the CLI yet.
close_reason: Implemented the temporary git recorder core with stdlib-only wrapper generation, sanitized JSONL trace records, cleanup helper, and fake-git unit tests.

title: add codex probe capture and replay diagnostics
description: Implement opt-in tracing for gh/git probe behavior, summarize captured traces, add replay smoke checks, and validate compatibility with Codex-driven gh-forgejo-shim usage.
close_reason: Added opt-in gh trace capture, trace summaries, Codex smoke probes, temporary git recorder CLI/docs/tests, and consolidated turn documentation for probe-driven diagnostics.

title: Surface Forgejo PR URL for Codex.app
description: Codex.app is not seeing the Forgejo pull request link from the gh shim output. Diagnose the exact output contract Codex.app expects, add a red-capable regression test around the shimmed PR creation path, and update the implementation so the Forgejo PR URL is exposed in the right place.
close_reason: Default gh pr create output now includes a Codex.app-recognized marker while preserving Forgejo URLs and JSON output.

title: Make branch discovery and creation feel first class
description: Codex.app and similar GitHub-oriented GUIs can show incomplete branch lists or a disabled create-branch action until the branch picker is reopened. Investigate the local Git and gh-compatible surfaces these tools use, then add GitHub-shaped branch list/create compatibility where this shim can make Forgejo repositories behave more like GitHub.
close_reason: Added GitHub-shaped gh api branch/ref routing, Forgejo branch client methods, docs, tests, and turn documentation for first-class branch discovery and create-ref compatibility.

title: Refresh README for current shim capabilities
description: Update README.md so it reflects the current bootstrap, auth, GUI PATH, PR checks, issue, repo metadata, and rollback behavior.
close_reason: README now reflects the current bootstrap, auth, GUI PATH, PR checks, issue, repo metadata, config, rollback, and development behavior.

title: Add native Forgejo auth helpers
description: Implement auth login/import/status/logout commands so gh-forgejo-shim can store Forgejo tokens in user-local auth storage for Terminal and GUI-launched clients.
close_reason: Implemented native auth login/import/status/logout with validated shim-owned token storage, docs, tests, and turn documentation.

title: map forgejo ci status into github shaped checks
description: Translate Forgejo commit statuses into GitHub CLI-shaped PR status/check output so GitHub-oriented tools can read statusCheckRollup and gh pr checks for Forgejo repositories.
close_reason: Mapped Forgejo commit statuses into GitHub-shaped statusCheckRollup and gh pr checks output, with tests and documentation.

title: Broaden gh compatibility for probed commands
description: Add shim compatibility for additional gh commands commonly probed by developer tools: pr checks, pr diff, pr comment, pr checkout, issue list/view/create, and richer repo view JSON fields.
close_reason: Implemented Forgejo-backed gh compatibility for probed PR, issue, and repo view commands with tests and docs.

title: Add bootstrap command and gfj alias
description: Add a gh-forgejo-shim bootstrap command that diagnoses and repairs common setup gaps, add a shorter gfj console alias, and refresh README onboarding.
close_reason: implemented bootstrap command, gfj alias, README onboarding, tests, and turn documentation

title: Broaden README compatibility documentation beyond PRs
description: Update README.md so new users understand the full GitHub-style tool compatibility setup for Forgejo repositories, including repository discovery, origin/default-branch metadata, branch tracking, commit/push UI, repo metadata, and PR workflows. Keep the guidance general for Codex.app, T3 Code, and similar tools rather than PR-only or Codex-only.
close_reason: Broadened README.md to document GitHub-style tool compatibility beyond PR workflows, including repository state, default branch, upstream tracking, commit/push UI, repo metadata, GUI PATH, and auth discovery.

title: Document Codex origin remote requirements
description: Codex.app PR status probes expect common Git remote metadata. Document the setup new Forgejo users may need, including an origin remote alias, fetched origin refs, origin/HEAD, and branch tracking.
close_reason: Documented GitHub-style origin remote requirements for Forgejo checkouts in README.md.

title: Make GitHub CLI discovery robust for GUI-launched apps
description: Codex.app and similar GUI-launched tools can run with a minimal macOS PATH, causing gh-forgejo-shim integrations to report that GitHub CLI is unavailable even when Homebrew gh is installed. Add package-level discovery/fallback behavior and documentation so users do not need a local launchctl workaround.
close_reason: Added fallback GitHub CLI discovery, macOS GUI PATH install/uninstall commands, doctor checks, docs, tests, and turn documentation for GUI-launched app PATH failures.

title: Move Diffs SSR instruction to global AGENTS
description: The Diffs SSR requirement was added to this repository AGENTS.md, but it is a global documentation preference. Remove the repo-local rule, add it to the global Codex AGENTS.md, and update turn documentation accordingly.
close_reason: Moved the Diffs SSR requirement from repo-local AGENTS.md into the global Codex AGENTS.md and documented the change.

title: Relax shim PATH doctor check
description: Update doctor so it warns only when another gh executable resolves before the managed shim, instead of requiring ~/.local/bin to be the first PATH entry.
close_reason: Doctor now checks whether the managed shim is the first gh executable in PATH instead of requiring its directory to be first.

title: phase 08 worker a: port shim lifecycle commands
description: Implement the Rust install-shim and uninstall-shim lifecycle behavior for Phase 08, including managed-file marker checks, refusal to overwrite or remove unmanaged files, executable mode, and rollback-compatible marker behavior. Scope is bounded to shim lifecycle modules and tests.
notes: Worker A implementation added Rust shim lifecycle APIs and CLI dispatch for install-shim/uninstall-shim, with managed marker/refusal/force/uninstall tests. rustfmt and git diff --check pass for Worker A files. Cargo verification is blocked by concurrent non-Worker-A work: cli.rs currently calls missing run_doctor/run_bootstrap helpers and bootstrap.rs has empty-array type inference errors in BootstrapCheck::new calls at lines 86, 219, 240, and 266.
close_reason: integrated shim lifecycle commands, marker/refusal behavior, and tests into phase 08

title: phase 07 worker a: port Rust PR create
description: Port gh pr create/new parsing, body handling, fill behavior, and created URL output to the Rust runtime while preserving Python compatibility for Forgejo-backed workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Rust handles gh pr create/new with title/body/body-file/base/head/repo/fill/web/draft/json behavior using fake Forgejo tests; default output keeps the Codex-compatible created PR URL marker.
close_reason: Implemented and verified Rust PR create/new parsing, body handling, fill behavior, and Codex-compatible created URL output in Phase 07.

title: phase 09: package and release Rust binaries
description: Replace Python packaging with Rust-first distribution: native install layout, Homebrew or cargo-binstall path, CI builds, signing/checksums, migration from old pipx installs, and rollback. PyPI is not the target; any temporary PyPI migration package requires a separate decision bead and dated removal follow-up.
notes:
Packaging should be Rust-first. PyPI is not the target; any temporary PyPI migration package requires a decision bead and dated removal follow-up.
Subagents: split artifacts, CI/release workflows, installer path, and migration docs. Any PyPI proposal needs a decision bead.
close_reason: Implemented Phase 09 Rust-first packaging and release path: Cargo release metadata, reproducible release archive script, CI and tag release workflows, Homebrew formula template, install docs, pipx migration guide, checksums, and local clean-install gfj doctor smoke verification.

title: phase 08: port diagnostics and setup commands
description: Port management and diagnostic commands to Rust: install-shim, uninstall-shim, bootstrap, doctor, install-gui-path, uninstall-gui-path, trace summarize/smoke, and git-recorder.
notes:
Implementation order 08. Depends on phases 03, 04, and 05. Can run in parallel with phase 07 after its prerequisites are complete.
Subagents: split shim lifecycle, doctor/bootstrap, trace tools, and macOS GUI PATH; use a verifier for macOS behavior.
close_reason: ported Rust diagnostics/setup commands with tests and manual validation notes

title: phase 07: port mutating workflows
description: Port write workflows to Rust: pr create/new/comment/checkout, issue create/new, auth login/import/logout, token validation, branch/head/base handling, and safe failure messages.
notes:
Implementation order 07. Depends on phase 06. Ports create/comment/checkout/login/write paths after read-only behavior is stable.
Subagents: split by mutation family; keep checkout/git subprocess behavior separate; use fake servers and temp repos only.
close_reason: Phase 07 mutating workflows ported to Rust and verified with fake Forgejo APIs, temporary git repos, Rust/Python gates, compileall, and diff checks.

title: Add developer testing guide
description: Document the recommended testing workflow for gh-forgejo-shim developers, including fast source-based checks, installed-wrapper checks, pipx reinstall guidance, and Codex.app GUI validation.
close_reason: Added docs/dev/testing.html with the recommended source, installed-wrapper, and Codex.app testing workflows for developers.

title: Refresh Beads README for current project state
description: Update .beads/README.md so it reflects how this repository currently uses Beads, including recently completed gh-forgejo-shim additions and remaining tracker workflow guidance.
close_reason: Updated .beads/README.md with current project-specific Beads workflow, recent shipped capabilities, remaining open issues, and session close guidance.

title: Implement real filters for shimmed gh pr list search flags
description: gh pr list now accepts author, label, assignee, app, and search flags to avoid falling through or failing early, but those filters are not yet applied to Forgejo API results. Add real filtering or explicit unsupported-flag behavior once the needed Forgejo API semantics are confirmed.

title: Add live Forgejo integration coverage
description: Add an integration test path that exercises Forgejo-compatible REST responses through a local HTTP fixture or disposable Forgejo test instance, so API behavior is validated beyond unit-level fakes.

title: Add package release automation
description: Add CI and release automation for building, checking, and publishing gh-forgejo-shim once the project is ready for PyPI distribution.
close_reason: Implemented Rust-first package release automation with CI checks, GitHub release tarballs, checksums, artifact smoke tests, and Homebrew packaging docs; PyPI is intentionally not the target channel.

title: phase 10: cut over and remove Python runtime
description: Switch installed gh/gfj/gh-forgejo-shim commands to Rust, verify GitHub and Forgejo Codex workflows, remove Python source/runtime packaging, audit any remaining Python through separate decision beads, and update docs for the final Rust-only product.
notes:
Final phase target is zero Python. Any retained Python requires a separate decision bead and must not be installed runtime code.
Subagents: use verifiers only. One owner performs deletion/cutover; verifiers cover GitHub, Forgejo, remaining Python audit, and docs.
